rows.yaml:
collection: General
playbooks:
  - name: Manual Approve or Reject
    description: Manual trigger that pauses for analyst approval, then stamps the chosen verdict.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Start
        next: Analyst Decision

      - type: manual_input
        name: Analyst Decision
        arguments:
          title: "Approve or Reject?"
          description: "Review the details and choose an action."
        options:
          - display: Approve
            primary: true
            next: Set Approved Verdict
          - display: Reject
            next: Set Rejected Verdict

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Approved Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: approved
        next: End Approved

      - type: set_variable
        name: Set Rejected Verdict
        vars:
          verdict: rejected
        next: End Rejected

      - type: end
        name: End Approved

      - type: end
        name: End Rejected

        └─ Reject  ──► Record Rejection (set_variable + comment)  ──► End Rejected

rows.yaml:
Let me check whether this connector actually exists before authoring anything.
🚫 **Connector not found — playbook not authored.**

The connector `totally-made-up-connector` **does not exist** in the FortiSOAR connector catalog. The search returned zero matches, and the closest phonetic/fuzzy suggestions are completely unrelated connectors:

- `zscaler-client-connector`
- `otbase-inventory`
- `nmap-scanner`

None of those are what you described, so I'm **not fabricating a connector step** around a name I can't verify. Doing so would produce a playbook that silently fails at runtime (or fails to compile entirely) because the resolver validates connector identity and operation names against the live catalog.

---

## What to do next

1. **Verify the connector name.** Check *Settings → Connectors* in your FortiSOAR instance. The name in the catalog (not the display label) is what goes in `connector:`.

2. **Try a fuzzy search yourself.** If you know part of the real name, tell me and I'll re-run `find_connector` with that fragment.

3. **Check if it's installed but not configured.** A connector can be installed (visible in the catalog) but not yet configured (no active credential set). I can run `list_configured_connectors` to show only the ones that are fully active on your instance.

4. **If this is a custom/private connector**, share the connector's `info.json` or `operations.py` and I can author against its actual operation schema.

Once you confirm the real connector name and operation, I'll author the playbook correctly.

rows.yaml:
collection: SOC Response
playbooks:
  - name: FortiGate IP Block or Allow
    description: >
      Prompts an analyst to enter an IP address and choose Block or Allow.
      On Block, calls the FortiGate block_ip_new operation (Quarantine Based).
      On Allow, logs a note via set_variable.
    is_active: true
    steps:
      - type: start
        name: Start
        next: Get Analyst Input

      - type: manual_input
        name: Get Analyst Input
        arguments:
          title: "IP Address Action"
          description: "Enter the IP address you want to act on and select the desired action."
          inputs:
            - name: ip_address
              kind: ipv4
              label: IP Address
              required: true
              tooltip: "Enter the IPv4 address to block or allow (e.g. 192.168.1.10)"
            - name: action
              kind: select
              label: Action
              required: true
              options:
                - block
                - allow
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: Check Analyst Action
          - display: Allow
            next: Check Analyst Action

      - type: decision
        name: Check Analyst Action
        conditions:
          - display: Block
            when: "{{ vars.steps.Get_Analyst_Input.input.action == 'block' }}"
            next: Block IP on FortiGate
          - display: Allow
            default: true
            next: Log Allow Decision

      - type: connector
        name: Block IP on FortiGate
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: block_ip_new
          config: ""
          params:
            method: "Quarantine Based"
            ip_addresses: "{{ vars.steps.Get_Analyst_Input.input.ip_address }}"
            time_to_live: "6 Hour"
        next: Log Block Result

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Block Result
        vars:
          block_status: "Blocked {{ vars.steps.Get_Analyst_Input.input.ip_address }} on FortiGate. Result: {{ vars.steps.Block_IP_on_FortiGate }}"
        next: End

      - type: set_variable
        name: Log Allow Decision
        vars:
          allow_note: "Analyst chose to ALLOW {{ vars.steps.Get_Analyst_Input.input.ip_address }} — no block action taken."
        next: End

      - type: end
        name: End

live_tested.summary: playbook contains manual_input — /notrigger dry-run will block awaiting human input

rows.yaml:
collection: Phishing Response
playbooks:
  - name: Phishing Alert IP Triage
    description: >
      On phishing-tagged alert creation, scores every sender IP via VirusTotal.
      Prompts an analyst to block any high-score IPs (score > 50) via FortiGate,
      then posts a summary comment back to the alert.
    is_active: true
    steps:

      # ── 1. Trigger ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: start_on_create
        name: On Phishing Alert Created
        arguments:
          module: alerts
          when:
            logic: AND
            filters:
              - field: name
                op: contains
                value: phish
        next: Score Each Sender IP

      # ── 2. Score every IP in sender_ips (sequential for_each) ───────────
      # VT query_ip returns: { ip, country, detections, is_malicious, reputation }
      # where `reputation` is the numeric VirusTotal community score (int).
      - type: connector
        name: Score Each Sender IP
        for_each:
          item: "{{ vars.input.records[0].sender_ips }}"
          parallel: false
        arguments:
          connector: virustotal
          operation: query_ip
          config: ''
          params:
            ip: "{{ vars.item }}"
        next: Collect High Score IPs

      # ── 3. Extract IPs whose reputation score > 50 ──────────────────────
      # vars.steps.Score_Each_Sender_IP is the list of per-iteration result dicts.
      # Each dict carries the connector's output keys at top level.
      - type: set_variable
        name: Collect High Score IPs
        vars:
          # Full list of dicts for the summary comment
          scored_ips: "{{ vars.steps.Score_Each_Sender_IP | list }}"
          # Only the IP strings (not full dicts) for the FortiGate block call
          high_score_ips: >-
            {{ vars.steps.Score_Each_Sender_IP
               | selectattr('reputation', 'defined')
               | selectattr('reputation', 'ne', None)
               | list
               | selectattr('reputation', 'greaterthan', 50)
               | map(attribute='ip')
               | list }}
        next: Any High Score IP

      # ── 4. Branch: any IP above threshold? ──────────────────────────────
      - type: decision
        name: Any High Score IP
        conditions:
          - display: Score above 50 found
            when: "{{ vars.high_score_ips | length > 0 }}"
            next: Analyst Block Decision
          - display: All IPs clean
            default: true
            next: Post Summary Comment

      # ── 5. Ask analyst: Block or Skip ────────────────────────────────────
      - type: manual_input
        name: Analyst Block Decision
        arguments:
          title: "High-Reputation IPs Detected — Block?"
          description: >-
            The following sender IPs scored above 50 on VirusTotal:
            {{ vars.high_score_ips | join(', ') }}.
            Choose 'Block' to quarantine them via FortiGate (permanent
            quarantine), or 'Skip' to leave them unblocked.
          inputs:
            - name: analyst_action
              kind: select
              label: "Action"
              options:
                - Block
                - Skip
              required: true
        options:
          - display: Block
            primary: true
            next: Analyst Chose Block
          - display: Skip
            next: Analyst Chose Block

      # ── 6. Route on analyst's choice ─────────────────────────────────────
      - type: decision
        name: Analyst Chose Block
        conditions:
          - display: Block selected
            when: "{{ vars.steps.Analyst_Block_Decision.analyst_action == 'Block' }}"
            next: Block High Score IPs
          - display: Skip selected
            default: true
            next: Post Summary Comment

      # ── 7. Block each high-score IP via FortiGate quarantine ─────────────
      # Iterates over the plain-IP-string list built in step 3.
      - type: connector
        name: Block High Score IPs
        for_each:
          item: "{{ vars.high_score_ips }}"
          parallel: false
        arguments:
          connector: fortigate-firewall
          operation: block_ip_new
          config: ''
          params:
            method: Quarantine Based
            ip_addresses: "{{ vars.item }}"
            time_to_live: Never
        next: Post Summary Comment

      # ── 8. Post summary comment to the originating alert ─────────────────
      - type: set_variable
        name: Post Summary Comment
        vars:
          _noop: done
        message:
          content: >-
            ## Phishing Alert IP Triage Summary

            **Sender IPs scored by VirusTotal:**
            {% for entry in vars.steps.Score_Each_Sender_IP %}
            | IP | Reputation | Flag |
            |---|---|---|
            {% endfor %}
            {% for entry in vars.steps.Score_Each_Sender_IP %}
            | `{{ entry.ip }}` | {{ entry.reputation }} | {% if entry.reputation | int > 50 %}⚠️ HIGH{% else %}✅ OK{% endif %} |
            {% endfor %}

            **High-score IPs (score > 50):** `{{ vars.high_score_ips | join('`, `') or 'None' }}`

            **Action taken:** {{ vars.steps.Analyst_Block_Decision.analyst_action | default('N/A — no high-score IPs detected') }}
        next: End

      # ── 9. Terminal node ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
      - type: end
        name: End

live_tested.summary: trigger 'start_on_create' fires on real record events; /notrigger dry-run has no record context
